name: MikroTik hAP
title: MikroTik hAP
short_description: QCA9531-BL3A-R CPU, 64MB RAM, 5x LAN ports, 802.11b/g/n, RouterOS L4
description: The MikroTik hAP is the essential device for home and small office environments, offering seamless wireless connectivity and multiple wired connections. Building on the success of the RB951-2n, the hAP introduces several enhancements, making it an even more capable and efficient networking solution. Whether you need a robust wireless access point (AP) or just a few wired connections, the hAP delivers with simplicity and reliability. Equipped with a 2.4GHz AP, the MikroTik hAP adheres to the 802.11b/g/n wireless network standards, providing a radio rate of up to 300Mbps. This makes it great for bandwidth-intensive tasks like HD video streaming, large file transfers, and video conferencing.

detailed_description.heading: Compact & Versatile Home Access Point
detailed_description.text: The MikroTik hAP is the essential device for home and small office environments, offering seamless wireless connectivity and multiple wired connections. Building on the success of the RB951-2n, the hAP introduces several enhancements, making it an even more capable and efficient networking solution. Whether you need a robust wireless access point (AP) or just a few wired connections, the hAP delivers with simplicity and reliability. Equipped with a 2.4GHz AP, the MikroTik hAP adheres to the 802.11b/g/n wireless network standards, providing a radio rate of up to 300Mbps. This makes it great for bandwidth-intensive tasks like HD video streaming, large file transfers, and video conferencing.

detailed_description.heading: Simplified Setup & Use
detailed_description.text: One of the standout qualities of the MikroTik hAP is its user-friendly setup, designed with preconfigured settings that eliminate the need for a complex installation process. The device comes ready with default network settings such as SSID and security protocols, allowing users to quickly deploy it in their home or office environments. This simplicity and ease of use make the MikroTik hAP ideal for those who may not have extensive technical knowledge. All you need to do is connect the internet cable and power supply, and you’re ready to go, creating an instant wireless network with minimal effort. This plug-and-play approach is perfect for users who prefer minimal setup hassle, enabling you to start using your internet connection almost immediately. This significantly enhances user convenience, reduces setup time, and minimizes the need for technical support. Such devices are especially beneficial in environments where quick deployment is necessary, such as small offices, home networks, or temporary setups like conferences or events.

detailed_description.heading: Reliable Performance & Durability
detailed_description.text: Under the hood, the MikroTik hAP is powered by the robust QCA9531 chipset, designed by Qualcomm Atheros. This chipset integrates a powerful single-core CPU clocked at 650 MHz, providing ample processing power for typical home and small office networking tasks, including routing, firewall management, and bandwidth shaping. The hAP is complemented by 64 MB of RAM and 16 MB of storage memory, ensuring efficient handling of network traffic, multiple user connections, and running additional services like DHCP, DNS, and VPN. This configuration allows the device to operate smoothly and efficiently, even under demanding conditions. The MikroTik hAP’s maximum power consumption is just 5 W, making it an energy-efficient option for continuous operation. It is designed to operate within a wide temperature range from -30 °C to 70 °C, making it suitable for various environments, including outdoor installations with appropriate housing and areas with significant temperature fluctuations. This capability ensures that the device maintains consistent performance and longevity, even in challenging conditions. One of the standout features of the MikroTik hAP is its flexible power options. The device can be powered either through a DC plug with dimensions of 2.1 x 5.5 mm or via passive Power over Ethernet (PoE) from a PoE injector. This flexibility is particularly useful in environments where power outlets may be limited. The inclusion of passive PoE allows the device to receive both power and data over a single Ethernet cable, simplifying the installation process and reducing cable clutter.
